opp.name: Heifer_S2C_March26
opp.next_step: Send Carlas follow-up collateral focused on eSourcing, Contract Management, and Supplier Information Management. Position the next interaction as a deeper dive into the two modules she showed interest in: eSourcing and Contract Management. Reconnect with Carlas in approximately 3 months, targeting July or August. Invite Heifer’s IT team to the next discussion to assess Unit4 Agresso API/integration feasibility. Prepare a clearer pricing discussion by module and by user type before the next call. Be ready to map users across requisitioners, approvers, procurement, AP, and any goods-receipt stakeholders. Keep the opportunity framed as exploratory and nonprofit-paced, not near-term closing. Focus the follow-up narrative on replacing manual RFP processes currently handled through Excel and email. Validate whether a broader internal review or formal RFP may emerge after Carlas reviews materials. Use the next meeting to confirm budget path, stakeholder map, and implementation readiness.
opp.ais_why:
• Mar 18 intro positioned AI-driven eSourcing cutting RFP creation from 40 days to 5-6 days as a headline; contract management pitch centered on AI clause scanning and dynamic alerts.
• Heifer's Carlas Smith framed the engagement as exploratory with a nonprofit-paced timeline; AI features were demoed but interest stayed in eSourcing, CLM, and SIM modules.
• Single 37-min recorded discovery with follow-up explicitly deferred 3 months to July-August; sparse pipeline plus exploratory framing limits confidence.

items.area: No quantified ROI or value case (current RFP cycle time, cost, error rate at Heifer not measured)

sources: Avoma discovery call 2026-03-18, Carlas Smith: 'we do that manually today… regularly'; Bailey: '40 days to create an RFP, on average … drop down to about 5, 6 days '
metrics.narrative: Carlas articulated a qualitative operational pain: manual RFP creation via Excel and email. Zycus cited a 40-day manual RFP cycle vs 5–6 days with the platform (Hack Group study, Bailey Erazo, 2026-03-18 call), but Carlas did not quantify Heifer's own current cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or savings target. She confirmed Heifer runs RFPs 'regularly' and does 'manual' collaboration today (2026-03-18), signaling real pain but no dollar value or ROI case stated. The call concluded before financial impact was quantified. To advance, the deal team must work with Carlas to measure Heifer's current RFP volume, time-per-event, FTE cost, and build a defensible ROI model anchored to her finance and procurement reality.

sources: Avoma 2026-03-18 call, Carlas questions and interest confirmation
No Salesforce Task showing follow-up materials sent or reconnect scheduled
champion.narrative: Carlas Smith (Procurement Manager) is the sole identified champion. She is actively engaged: she took the discovery call on 2026-03-18, asked detailed, technical questions (ERP integration, multi-country support, contract alerts, e-signing, pricing per module vs suite), and requested follow-up materials and a July/August reconnect to review with her IT team. She explicitly stated interest in eSourcing and Contract Management. However, she has NO access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker (she said budget is 'not readily available' and deferred to a year-long nonprofit approval process), and she has not introduced Zycus to finance, IT, or executive stakeholders. Her engagement has gone cold: the planned follow-up materials were never logged as sent, and the July/August reconnect is now overdue (today is 2026-06-26, 99 days since the call). Champion strength is developing but at risk of fading due to zero follow-through from Zycus.

sources: Avoma 2026-03-18, Carlas: 'we do that manually today'; Bailey: 'most nonprofits… an RFP kicks off… the exec team likes to see all options'
competition.narrative: No competitor named by Carlas or identified in any Salesforce field or task. She did not mention an incumbent platform, another vendor under evaluation, or a status-quo/do-nothing preference beyond the current manual Excel/email process. Bailey asked 'is there any tools you guys use like this today?' and Carlas replied 'we do that manually today,' indicating no current S2P or eSourcing platform. Whether Heifer will run a competitive RFP (as Bailey suggested is common for nonprofits: 'the board or the exec team likes to see all options') or sole-source Zycus is unknown. Carlas said 'a broader internal review or formal RFP may emerge after I review materials,' so a competitive process is possible but not confirmed. The current competitive threat is DO NOTHING / stay manual, not a named vendor.

sources: Avoma 2026-03-18, Carlas: 'we do that manually today… Excel or email… regularly'; her question on contract alerts and thresholds
identify_pain.narrative: Carlas confirmed Heifer's procurement team manually creates RFPs via Excel and email collaboration ('we do that manually today… regularly'), a time-consuming, error-prone process Bailey benchmarked at ~40 days industry average. She also expressed interest in Contract Management to gain visibility into spend against contract thresholds and expiration alerts ('for every purchase order I do against the contract, does it alert me that I am about to reach x threshold or the contract is about to expire?'), indicating a current gap in contract compliance and spend tracking. The pain owner is Carlas Smith, Procurement Manager, who owns RFP creation and contract oversight. The pain is operational (manual, slow RFP cycles) and compliance-related (no dynamic contract alerts), but not yet quantified in time, cost, or risk terms.

sources: Avoma 2026-03-18, Bailey: 'about 3 to 4 months for integrating… source to contract'; Carlas asked about e-signing, multi-country deployment
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, InfoSec, or procurement channel mechanics discussed on the 2026-03-18 call. Carlas asked whether e-signing is done 'in Zycus' or via DocuSign/Adobe Sign, and Bailey confirmed both are supported, but no MSA, SOW, legal-review process, or InfoSec questionnaire was mentioned. Heifer operates in 18 countries; a global rollout likely requires data-residency, privacy (GDPR/local-law), and security reviews, but none of these were scoped. Implementation was estimated at 3–4 months for the three modules (eSourcing, Contract Management, Supplier Information Management) by Bailey, but no formal project plan, sponsor, or implementation partner identified. This will emerge only after budget and stakeholder map are confirmed.

sources: Avoma 2026-03-18, Carlas: 'exploratory… not gonna be funds readily available… that's normally a year process'
SF contact roster (account-level, not opp contact roles): CFO Marcia Rasmussen, VP Global Finance Francine Hill — neither contacted
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified. The only buyer participant on the 2026-03-18 call was Carlas Smith, Procurement Manager. She stated this is 'exploratory' and 'that's normally a year process within itself to a nonprofit… not gonna be funds readily available just to say yes,' signaling she does NOT control budget authority and would escalate to a higher approver. She did not name who approves budget. The Salesforce contact roster includes a CFO (Marcia Rasmussen) and a VP of Global Finance Operations (Francine Hill), but neither has been engaged on this opportunity. Until the CFO or equivalent finance executive is identified, mapped, and engaged on the business case, this deal cannot advance to commercial or contracting stages.

sources: Avoma 2026-03-18, Carlas: 'year process… not funds readily available… bring in my IT group… review your stuff'
Bailey's Next Step log (SF): 'Use the next meeting to confirm budget path, stakeholder map, and implementation readiness'
decision_process.narrative: Carlas described a nonprofit, budget-constrained, exploratory process: 'that's normally a year process within itself to a nonprofit' and 'most nonprofits… what it looks like is… an RFP kicks off in the coming months… the board or the exec team likes to see all options' (Bailey restating the pattern, Carlas agreeing, 2026-03-18). She stated she needs to review materials first, then 'bring in my IT group' to assess API integration, then validate costing and user-type pricing before any budget conversation. She did not name approval steps, stakeholder count, or board involvement explicitly. The planned July/August reconnect (now overdue) was intended as a checkpoint to 'confirm budget path, stakeholder map, and implementation readiness.' No approval chain or timeline formalized yet; procurement is leading discovery, finance and IT not yet engaged.

sources: Avoma 2026-03-18, Carlas questions on ERP integration, multi-country/currency, contract alerts, e-signing; her statement 'I would need to probably do a deeper dive on the modules… and to understand more of the costing'
decision_criteria.narrative: Carlas explicitly requested eSourcing (to replace manual Excel/email RFP workflows) and Contract Management (repository, dynamic alerts on spend thresholds and expiration dates, AI clause scanning, e-signing). She confirmed interest in Supplier Information Management as 'the brains behind everything' after Bailey positioned it as foundational (2026-03-18). She asked detailed questions about Unit4 Agresso ERP integration via open APIs, multi-country deployment (Heifer operates in 18 countries), multi-currency support, and whether the platform works 'in other countries.' These are confirmed must-haves. What remains unknown: scoring/weighting of criteria, formal RFP scoring rubric, security/InfoSec requirements, change-management priorities, and whether Heifer will run a competitive evaluation or sole-source. Carlas said she may bring the IT team to the next call to assess API feasibility and that 'a broader internal review or formal RFP may emerge after I review materials' — the decision process is not yet locked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS score, status, or 'why' recorded in Salesforce. The 2026-03-18 call focused on operational pain (manual RFPs, contract compliance) rather than AI/digital transformation appetite. Carlas asked functional questions but did not express excitement about AI or Merlin. Given the nonprofit context ('year process… not funds readily available') and the focus on replacing manual Excel workflows, Heifer appears AI Curious at best: open to automation if it solves a clear operational problem and fits the budget, but not leading with AI strategy or transformation.

items.name: Carlas Smith

items.title: Procurement Manager
items.sentiment: Positive interest in eSourcing and Contract Management; requested follow-up and reconnect

items.detail: Carlas stated 'not gonna be funds readily available just to say yes… that's normally a year process within itself to a nonprofit.' No CFO or finance executive engaged, no budget confirmed, no fiscal-year timing established. $120k for three modules may require board approval and a formal budgeting cycle.

Close date 2027-02-26 (8 months out) is a placeholder. Carlas framed this as exploratory with a year-long nonprofit approval process. No formal timeline or project plan established. The July/August reconnect (now overdue) was the first checkpoint; missed deadlines signal low priority.

Single-threaded through Carlas (Procurement Manager) who has no budget authority and has not introduced Zycus to finance, IT, or executive stakeholders. No CFO, VP Finance, or Director IT engaged. No executive sponsor identified.

Unit4 Agresso ERP integration via open APIs is required and was confirmed as feasible by Bailey, but Heifer's IT team has not been engaged to validate API availability, scoping, or implementation effort. Carlas said 'we might even have an opportunity to bring in my IT group' but this has not happened.

Competitive RFP risk: Bailey stated 'most nonprofits… an RFP kicks off in the coming months… the board or the exec team likes to see all options,' and Carlas said 'a broader internal review or formal RFP may emerge after I review materials.' Heifer may run a competitive multi-vendor evaluation, and Zycus has no incumbent advantage or champion access to the decision committee.

Ghost risk: 99 days dark, zero follow-through on committed deliverable (follow-up collateral), planned reconnect overdue. Carlas may have deprioritized this, moved on to another vendor, or lost internal sponsorship. No signal of continued interest.

Champion strength was developing but is now at high risk of fading due to zero follow-through.

items.expected_effect: Restart the relationship before Carlas moves on; apologize for delay, deliver the promised materials, and lock a calendar date for the IT-inclusive reconnect call within the next 2 weeks. If no reply within 5 business days, this deal is cold and should be disqualified or escalated.

items.action: Gather Heifer's current RFP volume, cycle time, and FTE cost to build a nonprofit-relevant ROI model before the reconnect call
items.trigger: Carlas confirmed Heifer runs RFPs 'regularly' via manual Excel/email workflows (2026-03-18), but no quantified metrics captured (time, cost, error rate); she stated she needs 'to understand more of the costing' and will not get 'funds readily available' without a business case
items.expected_effect: Enable Carlas to build an internal business case anchored to Heifer's reality: X RFPs/year at Y days each = Z FTE hours saved → $A reallocated to mission vs admin overhead. Position Zycus as a mission-efficiency investment, not a cost center, to unlock CFO and board support.

items.expected_effect: Build the multi-thread and engage the power roles (CFO for budget, IT for integration feasibility, finance VP as potential executive sponsor). Without finance and IT in the room, this deal cannot advance past exploratory. Add all engaged stakeholders as OpportunityContactRoles immediately after the call.

items.action: On the reconnect call, establish Heifer's decision process including approval steps, board involvement, and fiscal-year timing

items.expected_effect: Map the approval path (CFO → board? competitive RFP? sole-source?) and anchor the timeline to Heifer's fiscal year and budget cycle. This will determine whether the 2027-02-26 close date is realistic or needs to slip to align with their process.

items.action: Prepare a nonprofit reference case showing ROI and change-management success at a peer organization
items.trigger: Carlas framed this as exploratory ('year process… not funds readily available'); nonprofits are risk-averse and budget-constrained; she will likely need board and CFO buy-in; a peer success story de-risks the investment
items.expected_effect: Provide social proof that a similar-sized international nonprofit achieved measurable ROI (time saved, cost reduction, compliance improvement) and successfully implemented Zycus within their budget and change-management constraints. Position this as part of the follow-up collateral to strengthen Carlas's internal pitch.

north_star_verdict.headline: 99 days dark after single exploratory call. Rep failed to send promised materials or schedule July/August reconnect as buyer requested. No champion, no EB, zero contact for 14 weeks. Deal is dead.

best_practice_check.summary: 99 days dark with zero follow-through on a committed deliverable; single-threaded; no Economic Buyer or multi-stakeholder engagement; no ROI case built; competitor intelligence unknown (may run RFP)

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor. The competitive threat is DO NOTHING / remain on manual Excel and email RFP workflows. Carlas did not name an incumbent platform or another vendor under evaluation. She stated Heifer does RFPs 'manually today' and expressed interest in Zycus eSourcing and Contract Management, but framed the conversation as exploratory with a potential 'broader internal review or formal RFP' to follow. Whether Heifer will run a competitive multi-vendor evaluation (common in nonprofits per Bailey) or sole-source is unknown. Current threat level: MEDIUM (inertia and budget constraints in a nonprofit are the real blocker, not a peer vendor).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI capabilities were presented on the 2026-03-18 call: AI-driven RFP template generation, AI clause scanning in Contract Management, autonomous negotiation agents, and Supplier Information Management as 'the brains behind everything.' Carlas did not explicitly react to the AI features; her interest was functional (replacing manual workflows, contract alerts). No AIS score recorded in Salesforce. The Merlin product is listed in Products__c, but it was not explicitly positioned or discussed on the call. AI positioning was present but not a differentiator in this early exploratory conversation.

items.quote: we do that manually today… regularly… Is this also inclusive of RFPs… term of references, TORs, things of that nature? [Bailey confirmed yes, with AI-driven templates, 40→5–6 day cycle reduction]
items.criterion: eSourcing to replace manual Excel/email RFP collaboration

items.quote: for every purchase order I do against the contract, does it alert me that I am about to reach x threshold or the contract is about to expire? [Bailey: 'you can set rules or thresholds for dollar amounts as well as dates… notify me that I need to get it renewed']

items.criterion: Contract Management with dynamic spend-threshold and expiration alerts

items.quote: Is this platform usable in other countries? [Bailey: 'Yep. Absolutely… locations throughout the globe… multiple locations, totally okay… multiple ERPs or multiple languages… Currency. All of that.']
items.source: Avoma, Carlas question and Bailey confirmation
items.criterion: Multi-country deployment (Heifer operates in 18 countries) with multi-currency support

items.quote: What ERP system do you guys use? [Bailey: 'as long as Unit 4 has open APIs, which pretty much every ERP system does, we can integrate with it']

items.quote: I would need to probably do a deeper dive on the modules… and to understand more of the costing for it… not gonna be funds readily available just to say yes… that's normally a year process within itself to a nonprofit

items.quote: I usually see about 3 to 4 months for integrating… for for implementation
items.criterion: Implementation timeline (3–4 months for 3 modules) fits nonprofit 'year process'

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Heifer's stated needs (eSourcing to replace manual RFP workflows, Contract Management with dynamic alerts, multi-country/currency support, Unit4 Agresso ERP integration) align well with Zycus platform capabilities. Bailey confirmed API integration, global deployment, multi-currency, and 3–4 month implementation. The gap is pricing clarity (Carlas needs per-module and per-user-type costing) and proof that the platform works at nonprofit scale and pace. Fit is aligned on functionality, partially aligned on budget/timing (exploratory, year-long process).

• Mar 18 intro positioned AI-driven eSourcing cutting RFP creation from 40 days to 5-6 days as a headline; contract management pitch centered on AI clause scanning and dynamic alerts.
• Heifer's Carlas Smith framed the engagement as exploratory with a nonprofit-paced timeline; AI features were demoed but interest stayed in eSourcing, CLM, and SIM modules.
• Single 37-min recorded discovery with follow-up explicitly deferred 3 months to July-August; sparse pipeline plus exploratory framing limits confidence.

deltas.subject: RE-ENGAGE IMMEDIATELY: the July/August reconnect Carlas requested is now overdue (today 2026-06-26, call was 2026-03-18, ~14 weeks ago), and the follow-up collateral Bailey committed to send was never logged as delivered. The deal owner must reach out this week with an apology for the delay, send the promised materials, and propose a specific date in the next 10 days to reconnect with Carlas and invite her IT team. If no reply within 5 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-level re-engagement.

deltas.to: BUILD THE MULTI-THREAD: Carlas is the sole contact (0 buyer-side contact roles on this opp). Heifer is a $120k opportunity operating in 18 countries with a year
deltas.subject: BUILD THE MULTI-THREAD: Carlas is the sole contact (0 buyer-side contact roles on this opp). Heifer is a $120k opportunity operating in 18 countries with a year-long nonprofit decision process; a single procurement manager cannot carry this deal to close. Use the reconnect call to ask Carlas to introduce the CFO or VP of Global Finance Operations (Francine Hill or Marcia Rasmussen per account contacts), the Director of IT (Cedric Lambert per account contacts), and the VP-level sponsor who will champion the business case internally. Add all engaged stakeholders as OpportunityContactRoles immediately.

deltas.to: QUANTIFY THE ROI AND BUILD THE BUSINESS CASE: Carlas confirmed Heifer runs RFPs 'regularly' via manual Excel/email workflows, but no time, cost, or error metric
deltas.subject: QUANTIFY THE ROI AND BUILD THE BUSINESS CASE: Carlas confirmed Heifer runs RFPs 'regularly' via manual Excel/email workflows, but no time, cost, or error metrics were captured. Before the next call, work with Carlas (via email or brief pre-call) to gather: (a) how many RFPs Heifer runs per year, (b) average time per RFP today, (c) FTE cost of the procurement team involved, (d) error/rework rate. Use these inputs to build a nonprofit-relevant ROI model (time saved → more mission $ vs admin overhead) that the CFO and board will find credible. Present this on the reconnect call and position it as the foundation for her internal business case.

validation gate: hard.next_step 'Send Carlas follow-up collateral focused on eSourcing, Contract Management, and Supplier Information Management. Position the next interaction as a deeper dive into the two modules she showed interest in: eSourcing and Contract Management. Reconnect with Carlas in approximately 3 months, targeting July or August. Invite Heifer’s IT team to the next discussion to assess Unit4 Agresso API/integration feasibility. Prepare a clearer pricing discussion by module and by user type before the next call. Be ready to map users across requisitioners, approvers, procurement, AP, and any goods-receipt stakeholders. Keep the opportunity framed as exploratory and nonprofit-paced, not near-term closing. Focus the follow-up narrative on replacing manual RFP processes currently handled through Excel and email. Validate whether a broader internal review or formal RFP may emerge after Carlas reviews materials. Use the next meeting to confirm budget path, stakeholder map, and implementation readiness.' diverges from Salesforce '
